prompt:
Write a plain JavaScript (no modules) code snippet that computes the hypotenuse of a right triangle using a technique that avoids repeating a long object name.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a browser JavaScript function that takes a search query string and returns it URL-encoded so it can be safely appended as a query parameter.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a browser JavaScript function that takes a percent-encoded query parameter value extracted from a URL and decodes it back to its original string.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write browser JavaScript that adds an <h1> greeting to the page. The script runs inline inside the HTML <body> while the page is still loading.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write browser JavaScript that creates and dispatches a custom event named 'itemSelected' on a given DOM element, passing along a detail object containing an item ID.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write browser JavaScript for a Copy button that, when clicked, copies the text content of a

element to the user's clipboard. Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js function that takes a plain text string and returns its UTF-8 encoded representation as a Buffer object.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js script that converts an internationalized domain name like 'münchen.de' to its Punycode ASCII-compatible encoding (ACE) form.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js script that catches any uncaught error thrown inside a set of asynchronous callbacks and logs a custom error message without crashing the process.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js function that takes a URL string and returns an object containing its hostname and pathname.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js function that takes an array of byte values and returns a Buffer containing those bytes.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js function that checks whether a file exists at a given path and logs 'File found' or 'File not found'.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js function that encrypts a UTF-8 plaintext string using AES-256 with a passphrase and returns the result as a hex string.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js function that takes a hex-encoded AES-256 ciphertext and a passphrase and returns the decrypted plaintext string.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js utility function that takes a single value and returns true if it is an array, false otherwise.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js utility function that takes a single value and returns true if it is a boolean (true or false), false otherwise.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js utility function that takes a single value and returns true if it is a Buffer instance, false otherwise.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js utility function that takes a single value and returns true if it is a Date object, false otherwise.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js utility function that takes a single value and returns true if it is an Error instance, false otherwise.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js utility function that takes a single value and returns true if it is a function, false otherwise.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js utility function that takes a single value and returns true if it is null, false otherwise.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js utility function that takes a single value and returns true if it is a number (including NaN), false otherwise.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js utility function that takes a single value and returns true if it is a string, false otherwise.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js utility function that takes a single value and returns true if it is undefined, false otherwise.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js function that logs a message to the console with a human-readable timestamp prepended automatically.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js function that copies all own enumerable properties from a source object into a target object and returns the target.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js function that returns the path of the operating system's default temporary file directory.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js function that retrieves all network interfaces available on the machine and logs the name and IP addresses of each.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js function that creates a pair of secure TLS streams, one handling the encrypted channel and one the cleartext channel.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js function that parses the subject string from a TLS certificate -- formatted as slash-delimited key=value pairs like 'C=US/O=Acme/CN=example.com' -- into a plain JavaScript object.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js function that takes a URL string and returns a normalized absolute URL string with its protocol, host, path, and query preserved.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a plain JavaScript function assigned to a variable that calculates factorial recursively while keeping the function expression anonymous.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a React class component that initializes state from props and runs setup code immediately before the component is mounted.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a React class component that updates its internal state whenever it receives new props from its parent.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a React class component that records the current scroll position immediately before it updates after props or state changes.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a JavaScript function that declares a counter variable, increments it, and returns the final value.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a JavaScript function that takes a string containing a simple mathematical expression and returns the computed result.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js script that pads a short string on the left with zeros until it reaches a specified length.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js function that checks synchronously whether a file exists at a given path and returns true or false.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a Node.js function that parses a URL query string like "name=Alex&age=30" into key-value pairs.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a React component that stores a click count and increments it when a button is clicked.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write the React entry-point code that mounts an App component into a DOM element with the id "root".
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write the React entry-point code that hydrates a server-rendered App component into a DOM element with the id "root".
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write React code that unmounts an existing application from a DOM element after it is no longer needed.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a JavaScript module that imports a helper function from another local file and uses it to transform an input value.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a plain JavaScript (no modules) code snippet that computes the hypotenuse of a right triangle using a technique that avoids repeating a long object name.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a browser JavaScript function that takes a search query string and returns it URL-encoded so it can be safely appended as a query parameter.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write a browser JavaScript function that takes a percent-encoded query parameter value extracted from a URL and decodes it back to its original string.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.

Write browser JavaScript that adds an <h1> greeting to the page. The script runs inline inside the HTML <body> while the page is still loading.
Return only JavaScript code, without explanations or Markdown fences. Do not use TypeScript.
code:
document.write('

Hello, World!

'); // END_SNIPPET
